content:
# Hydrodynamics

**Summary**: The study of the motion of fluids and the forces acting on them.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2015-barri|Barri, 2015]] evaluated the flow dynamics during wormhole creation using Damköhler numbers.

- [[2024-suleiman-2|Suleiman, 2024]] analyzed the displacement of brine from pores under pressure.

## Scientific Context

- [[2015-barri|Barri, 2015]] Used to determine the optimal balance between reaction rate and transport rate.

- [[2024-suleiman-2|Suleiman, 2024]] analyzed the displacement of brine from pores under pressure.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2015-barri|Barri, 2015]] Calculated from injection rates, pore volumes, and dissolution constants.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The optimal Damköhler number for most fluids was found to be approximately 0.29. ([[2015-barri|Barri, 2015, p. 37]])


- At 20 psi, a substantial portion of the brine is displaced from pores ranging from 7 to 10 μm in limestone. ([[2024-suleiman-2|Suleiman, 2024, p. 61]])
